task_completion.description: The house project is opened in the design software.
task_completion.visual_evidence: DreamPlan Home Design Software is open and displays a 3D house model.

task_completion.description: A wheelchair ramp is installed at the front entrance.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A ramp structure is visible specifically at the front entrance of the house.

task_completion.description: The ramp connects the ground level to the porch level.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bottom of the ramp rests on the ground/terrain, and the top of the ramp is flush with the porch floor.
The structure is a smooth incline suitable for a wheelchair, not a set of stairs or a steep drop.
Railings or handrails are visible along the sides of the installed ramp.

task_completion.description: A screenshot of the 3D view is exported to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or file explorer shows 'ramp_install.png' being saved to 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\', or the final image is the requested 3D view screenshot.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the DreamPlan GUI to create the ramp.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the DreamPlan interface with building tools, menus, or object properties open, rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: The ramp is a genuine 3D object in the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The ramp matches the lighting, perspective, and rendering style of the rest of the house, indicating it wasn't pasted in via an image editor.

integrity.description: The agent used the correct home design software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application window clearly belongs to DreamPlan Home Design Software.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected screenshot path is C:\Users\Docker\Documents\ramp_install.png. Note that while the task description mentions 'Sample House', the actual project loaded in the environment is named 'Contemporary House'.
